methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
SiPearl is a Series A-stage semiconductor company founded by Philippe Notton that has raised €130M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 4 same-sector, same-stage private companies in Chips & Semiconductors: Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), Substrate ($1.0B), and Rain AI ($500M).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation technique given SiPearl's private status and lack of disclosed revenue. The median valuation across these 4 comparables is $1.5B, with a range spanning $500M at the low end (Rain AI) to $5.0B at the high end (Etched). This method anchors the analysis directly to observable recent fundings in the identical sector and stage. Public semiconductor comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) trade at a median P/S of 24.4x, but cannot be applied without SiPearl's revenue figure, which remains undisclosed.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.3B to $1.7B, centered on the $1.5B peer median. Key uncertainties include relative product maturity and differentiation versus the named peers, customer traction and path to revenue, and the outcome of ongoing capital raises that may anchor a post-money valuation independently of this analysis. The single-method foundation reflects limited public data availability at this stage.
